unchangedJobs.title: Product Merchandising Intern - EMEA Internship Program
unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Product Merchandising Intern for The North Face . About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! As a Product Merchandising Intern you will support the end-to-end product lifecycle by translating market insights into compelling assortments that drive sales and brand growth. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure the right product is in the right place at the right time, maximizing customer impact and business performance. - Support the SMU (Special Make Up) line planning process by analyzing consumer insights, sales performance, and market trends to inform the FW/SS assortment strategy for Europe. - Coordinate the go-to-market process by managing timelines, tools, and product data accuracy to ensure seamless cross-functional alignment. - Monitor in-season product performance to identify risks and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to optimize sell-through and margin. - Collaborate with global and regional teams to ensure correct data flows into the systems & assortments reflect brand direction while meeting local market needs and commercial goals. - Contribute to visual line reviews and product presentations by supporting the preparation of compelling tools. - Support data entry of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) systems, support with transfer from PLM to Flex and update linelists to reflect correct information How you will make a difference To succeed as an Associate Product Merchandising Intern, you should be curious about products, eager to learn how assortments are built, and ready to support a fast-paced team with energy and precision. We are looking for someone who has demonstrated: - The ability to work with numbers and draw simple conclusions to support product decisions. - The capability to stay organized and manage multiple tasks with attention to detail. - A team player. Working x functionally is essential as a assistant product merchandiser. - An understanding of what makes a product appealing to consumers , especially in the European market. - Eye for detail to ensure accuracy across the tools & PLM systems - A basic knowledge of Excel or similar tools to help with data and product tracking. - A keen interest in fashion, retail, or product creation , and a desire to grow in this field. - A positive, can-do attitude , and a willingness to support the team wherever needed. What’s in it For You By joining our team, you will: - Learn how to build and manage a seasonal product line , from concept to market launch. - Gain exposure to real-time business decisions , using data to support product and commercial strategies. - Understand the role of merchandising within a global brand , and how it connects with marketing, sales, and product teams. - Develop your analytical and storytelling skills , turning numbers into product narratives. - Build a strong professional network , with opportunities to connect across teams, functions, and brands. - Receive mentorship and training , helping you grow your confidence and capabilities in a dynamic, international environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0020

Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure the right product is in the right place at the right time, maximizing customer impact and business performance. - Support the SMU (Special Make Up) line planning process by analyzing consumer insights, sales performance, and market trends to inform the FW/SS assortment strategy for Europe. - Coordinate the go-to-market process by managing timelines, tools, and product data accuracy to ensure seamless cross-functional alignment. - Monitor in-season product performance to identify risks and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to optimize sell-through and margin. - Collaborate with global and regional teams to ensure correct data flows into the systems & assortments reflect brand direction while meeting local market needs and commercial goals. - Contribute to visual line reviews and product presentations by supporting the preparation of compelling tools. - Support data entry of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) systems, support with transfer from PLM to Flex and update linelists to reflect correct information How you will make a difference To succeed as an Associate Product Merchandising Intern, you should be curious about products, eager to learn how assortments are built, and ready to support a fast-paced team with energy and precision. We are looking for someone who has demonstrated: - The ability to work with numbers and draw simple conclusions to support product decisions. - The capability to stay organized and manage multiple tasks with attention to detail. - A team player. Working x functionally is essential as a assistant product merchandiser. - An understanding of what makes a product appealing to consumers , especially in the European market. - Eye for detail to ensure accuracy across the tools & PLM systems - A basic knowledge of Excel or similar tools to help with data and product tracking. - A keen interest in fashion, retail, or product creation , and a desire to grow in this field. - A positive, can-do attitude , and a willingness to support the team wherever needed. What’s in it For You By joining our team, you will: - Learn how to build and manage a seasonal product line , from concept to market launch. - Gain exposure to real-time business decisions , using data to support product and commercial strategies. - Understand the role of merchandising within a global brand , and how it connects with marketing, sales, and product teams. - Develop your analytical and storytelling skills , turning numbers into product narratives. - Build a strong professional network , with opportunities to connect across teams, functions, and brands. - Receive mentorship and training , helping you grow your confidence and capabilities in a dynamic, international environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0020

unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Creative Designer Intern for The North Face. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) - Please make sure to upload both a CV and a portfolio ! Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! We are looking for Retail Creative Designer Intern to contribute to creative research, concept development, and graphic design for our The North Face retail spaces. The intern will work closely with the team to transform ideas into visual and spatial concepts, supporting projects from inspiration to execution. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Retail Creative Designer Intern for: - Developing 2D graphic layouts for brand campaign storytelling that engages, informs, and inspires our consumers - Design POP and strut cards for in store communication (for campaigns, promotions, special communications) - Co-work with 3D designer for the creative proposal for window & instore campaign execution. - Supporting for the design of graphic identity for retail activations and special events - Supporting the design of flagships executions and adapting window & instore executions for special locations. - Hoarding graphics design for new store openings - Support the development of new creative ideas like wall customisations, creation of corners and study new design elements to inject in our new stores TNF brand DNA as briefed by Retail Architects. - Support retail adaptations for Wholesale stores - Support the creation of the monthly retail marketing newsletter How you will make a difference - A degree in design, product design, interior design, architecture, or graphic design - Proficiency in 2D and 3D software for graphic design and modelling - Strong creativity in graphic design and concept development - Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) - Passion for creative research and innovation in graphic design and spatial development - Ability to envision and create compelling concepts, from mood boards to full spatial designs - Excellent attention to detail and team collaboration skills - Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills Curious mindset with passion for working with people What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: • Project coordination • Retail marketing principles • How to elevate brand representation instore • Manage retail marketing database (instore and window pictures, measurements, floor plans) • You will be expose to all the retail marketing team members, learning key principles of retail architectures, visual merchandising, retail activations and creative environment The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. - Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260210-0017

Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) - Please make sure to upload both a CV and a portfolio ! Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! We are looking for Retail Creative Designer Intern to contribute to creative research, concept development, and graphic design for our The North Face retail spaces. The intern will work closely with the team to transform ideas into visual and spatial concepts, supporting projects from inspiration to execution. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Retail Creative Designer Intern for: - Developing 2D graphic layouts for brand campaign storytelling that engages, informs, and inspires our consumers - Design POP and strut cards for in store communication (for campaigns, promotions, special communications) - Co-work with 3D designer for the creative proposal for window & instore campaign execution. - Supporting for the design of graphic identity for retail activations and special events - Supporting the design of flagships executions and adapting window & instore executions for special locations. - Hoarding graphics design for new store openings - Support the development of new creative ideas like wall customisations, creation of corners and study new design elements to inject in our new stores TNF brand DNA as briefed by Retail Architects. - Support retail adaptations for Wholesale stores - Support the creation of the monthly retail marketing newsletter How you will make a difference - A degree in design, product design, interior design, architecture, or graphic design - Proficiency in 2D and 3D software for graphic design and modelling - Strong creativity in graphic design and concept development - Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) - Passion for creative research and innovation in graphic design and spatial development - Ability to envision and create compelling concepts, from mood boards to full spatial designs - Excellent attention to detail and team collaboration skills - Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills Curious mindset with passion for working with people What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: • Project coordination • Retail marketing principles • How to elevate brand representation instore • Manage retail marketing database (instore and window pictures, measurements, floor plans) • You will be expose to all the retail marketing team members, learning key principles of retail architectures, visual merchandising, retail activations and creative environment The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. - Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260210-0017

unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Allocator Intern for Timberland. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you ready to turn data-driven decisions into winning retail strategies? Timberland is looking for a Retail Allocator intern to play a key role in ensuring the right product reaches the right store at the right time across EMEA. Support the allocation and replenishment of product to our EMEA retail stores by analysing sales trends, monitoring inventory levels, and ensuring accurate product flow to stores. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Retail allocator intern: - Report updating. - Manual allocation to stores. - Track stock levels, sales trends, and store performance to identify risks and opportunities, recommending adjustments to maximize in-season results. - Initial allocation set-up - Ad hoc analysis on products / performance. - Promotions upload in the system. - Warehouse logics and interaction. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - You enjoy working with data and transforming numbers into clear, meaningful insights that support better decision‑making across Allocation & Planning - You have strong problem‑solving skills , and you naturally look for patterns, root causes, and opportunities to improve processes. - You are detail‑oriented yet able to zoom out , balancing accuracy with a good understanding of the bigger commercial picture. - You are proactive and comfortable taking ownership , even when facing new or ambiguous tasks—your initiative helps move projects forward. - You are curious and eager to learn new tools , especially analytics platforms, reporting environments, and product systems (no prior expertise needed—just motivation). What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Deep understanding of retail environment across Europe - Interactions across the different department (planning, allocation, merchandising, operations, marketing) - Understand main logic of a specific category allocation, giving inputs on product needs. - Understand the differences across countries in regards to product trends. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260204-0003

unchangedJobs.title: Digital Trading Intern - EMEA Internship Program
unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Digital Trading Intern for Timberland. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Become the engine behind our Digital Trading performance. Step into a fast‑paced, data‑driven environment where your work will directly elevate how consumers shop our brand across eCommerce and Digital Marketplace partners. You will support and coordinate day‑to‑day digital trading activities to maximize visibility, conversion, and revenue across our owned eCommerce platforms and Digital Marketplace partners. Drive actionable insights, ensure flawless execution, and help optimize performance levers to accelerate digital growth. Furthermore, you will: - Monitor and update daily trading performance dashboards, surfacing insights that support decision‑making. - Support building and executing trading calendar and provide weekly/monthly updates to the extended cross-functional team. - Assist in demand & inventory reviews , helping identify risks and opportunities across key accounts and categories. - Conduct competitive benchmarking , identifying trends, best sellers, gaps, and potential growth levers. - Lead or support additional cross‑functional projects within the Digital Trading team to drive continuous improvement and operational excellence. How you will make a difference - Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate numbers into clear, actionable insights. - Curiosity for digital commerce, performance optimization, and online consumer behavior. - Attention to detail and a proactive approach to spotting issues before they become problems. - Comfort with fast‑paced environments, managing multiple tasks while keeping quality high. - Ability to collaborate effectively across teams such as merchandising, planning, marketing, and digital operations. - Problem‑solving attitude, always looking for smarter, more efficient ways of working. What’s in it for you - Hands‑on experience in Digital Trading , one of the fastest‑growing areas in the retail & brand ecosystem. - Exposure to eCommerce and Marketplace operations , from onsite execution to performance analysis. - Practical knowledge of performance metrics , dashboards, and commercial KPIs used by leading digital retailers. - Experience working cross‑functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and digital operations teams. - The opportunity to contribute to real‑world digital initiatives, building a strong foundation for a future career in digital retail, eCommerce, or marketplace management. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? descriptionByLocale.en: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Digital Trading Intern for Timberland. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Become the engine behind our Digital Trading performance. Step into a fast‑paced, data‑driven environment where your work will directly elevate how consumers shop our brand across eCommerce and Digital Marketplace partners. You will support and coordinate day‑to‑day digital trading activities to maximize visibility, conversion, and revenue across our owned eCommerce platforms and Digital Marketplace partners. Drive actionable insights, ensure flawless execution, and help optimize performance levers to accelerate digital growth. Furthermore, you will: - Monitor and update daily trading performance dashboards, surfacing insights that support decision‑making. - Support building and executing trading calendar and provide weekly/monthly updates to the extended cross-functional team. - Assist in demand & inventory reviews , helping identify risks and opportunities across key accounts and categories. - Conduct competitive benchmarking , identifying trends, best sellers, gaps, and potential growth levers. - Lead or support additional cross‑functional projects within the Digital Trading team to drive continuous improvement and operational excellence. How you will make a difference - Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate numbers into clear, actionable insights. - Curiosity for digital commerce, performance optimization, and online consumer behavior. - Attention to detail and a proactive approach to spotting issues before they become problems. - Comfort with fast‑paced environments, managing multiple tasks while keeping quality high. - Ability to collaborate effectively across teams such as merchandising, planning, marketing, and digital operations. - Problem‑solving attitude, always looking for smarter, more efficient ways of working. What’s in it for you - Hands‑on experience in Digital Trading , one of the fastest‑growing areas in the retail & brand ecosystem. - Exposure to eCommerce and Marketplace operations , from onsite execution to performance analysis. - Practical knowledge of performance metrics , dashboards, and commercial KPIs used by leading digital retailers. - Experience working cross‑functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and digital operations teams. - The opportunity to contribute to real‑world digital initiatives, building a strong foundation for a future career in digital retail, eCommerce, or marketplace management. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0011

unchangedJobs.title: PR & Marketing Intern - EMEA Internship Program
unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as PR & Brand Comms Intern for Timberland. ## About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . ## ## Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months, full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) ## Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA citizenship ## Let’s talk about the role! Are you passionate about storytelling and ready to shape how an iconic brand shows up across EMEA? We are looking for a PR & Marketing Intern to join TIMBERLAND within VF EMEA Internship Program. Drive impactful brand visibility by coordinating press materials, managing media interactions, and contributing to best‑in-class storytelling initiatives. ## ## Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for Supporting in Press Relations & Brand communications at EMEA level: - Daily interaction and exchange with countries Marketing Manager, agencies, TBL & VF internal functions and global team. - Support in defining structured EMEA seasonal PR roll out plans for the key markets managed by EMEA HQ - Support in PR activities managed by EMEA HQ (Media partnership, event management, editors’ seedings, activations) - Manage and deliver Global Press material to markets and wider TBL team (press releases and PR assets in files) - Develop press material (copywriting and proofing, distribution, ...) - Brand Calendar management: working with all internal and agencies: keep track of our key global/EMEA/ markets dates and key marketing styles throughout the season. - Sample management - support the marketing team with seasonal sample orders including tracking of deliveries as well as ad hoc sample requests (photo shooting e.t.c) - Attend and record notes at the PR team meetings and work cross-functionally to support PR team - Create several reports and presentations on various product launches and initiatives. - Maintain and update EMEA master media lists and seeding lists. - Reporting: performance and KPIs monitoring and deliveries of campaign/ monthly / quarterly reports - Respond to media requests for samples, information and press releases in a timely manner. - Work closely with Internal team to create synergies and increase visibility for Timberland (newsletter/ media alert/ …) - Build and maintain knowledge of competitive PR: Monitor competitor activities and share as required. ## What success looks like: Your skills in continuous improvement, decision making, planning & organizing, and customer focus – combined with your talent for collaboration, communication, and leveraging cultural diversity – will contribute to the team’s success. You are also adaptable in using applied learning; you initiate action and have high work standards. ## How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - You are fluent in English, with strong written communication skills and an instinct for brand storytelling. - You bring curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to thrive in fast‑paced, international environments. - You are proactive, well‑organized, and comfortable managing multiple tasks at once. - You are collaborative, culturally aware, and energized by connecting with people across teams and markets. - Previous experience through other internships, student projects, or extracurricular activities is considered a plus. ## A degree in Communications, Marketing, Fashion, Media or related fields is nice to have; what matters most is your passion and drive. ## What’s in it for you In this role, you will gain hands‑on experience at the intersection of PR, Marketing, and Brand Communication for one of the world’s most iconic outdoor lifestyle brands. At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have: - A deep understanding of how a global PR & Communications function operates in a multi‑market environment - Experience developing PR materials, managing press interactions, and supporting campaign execution - Strong skills in storytelling, content development, and cross‑functional coordination - Practical exposure to media relations, sample management, KPIs tracking, and PR reporting - A robust professional network across VF’s brands, teams, and markets ## The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12‑month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands‑on experience, including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. ## ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We celebrate the diversity of our associates and encourage everyone to bring their true selves to work every day. ## Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0011

We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands‑on experience, including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. We celebrate the diversity of our associates and encourage everyone to bring their true selves to work every day. ## Are you in? descriptionByLocale.de: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as PR & Brand Comms Intern for Timberland. ## About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . ## ## Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months, full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) ## Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA citizenship ## Let’s talk about the role! Are you passionate about storytelling and ready to shape how an iconic brand shows up across EMEA? We are looking for a PR & Marketing Intern to join TIMBERLAND within VF EMEA Internship Program. Drive impactful brand visibility by coordinating press materials, managing media interactions, and contributing to best‑in-class storytelling initiatives. ## ## Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for Supporting in Press Relations & Brand communications at EMEA level: - Daily interaction and exchange with countries Marketing Manager, agencies, TBL & VF internal functions and global team. - Support in defining structured EMEA seasonal PR roll out plans for the key markets managed by EMEA HQ - Support in PR activities managed by EMEA HQ (Media partnership, event management, editors’ seedings, activations) - Manage and deliver Global Press material to markets and wider TBL team (press releases and PR assets in files) - Develop press material (copywriting and proofing, distribution, ...) - Brand Calendar management: working with all internal and agencies: keep track of our key global/EMEA/ markets dates and key marketing styles throughout the season. - Sample management - support the marketing team with seasonal sample orders including tracking of deliveries as well as ad hoc sample requests (photo shooting e.t.c) - Attend and record notes at the PR team meetings and work cross-functionally to support PR team - Create several reports and presentations on various product launches and initiatives. - Maintain and update EMEA master media lists and seeding lists. - Reporting: performance and KPIs monitoring and deliveries of campaign/ monthly / quarterly reports - Respond to media requests for samples, information and press releases in a timely manner. - Work closely with Internal team to create synergies and increase visibility for Timberland (newsletter/ media alert/ …) - Build and maintain knowledge of competitive PR: Monitor competitor activities and share as required. ## What success looks like: Your skills in continuous improvement, decision making, planning & organizing, and customer focus – combined with your talent for collaboration, communication, and leveraging cultural diversity – will contribute to the team’s success. You are also adaptable in using applied learning; you initiate action and have high work standards. ## How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - You are fluent in English, with strong written communication skills and an instinct for brand storytelling. - You bring curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to thrive in fast‑paced, international environments. - You are proactive, well‑organized, and comfortable managing multiple tasks at once. - You are collaborative, culturally aware, and energized by connecting with people across teams and markets. - Previous experience through other internships, student projects, or extracurricular activities is considered a plus. ## A degree in Communications, Marketing, Fashion, Media or related fields is nice to have; what matters most is your passion and drive. ## What’s in it for you In this role, you will gain hands‑on experience at the intersection of PR, Marketing, and Brand Communication for one of the world’s most iconic outdoor lifestyle brands. At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have: - A deep understanding of how a global PR & Communications function operates in a multi‑market environment - Experience developing PR materials, managing press interactions, and supporting campaign execution - Strong skills in storytelling, content development, and cross‑functional coordination - Practical exposure to media relations, sample management, KPIs tracking, and PR reporting - A robust professional network across VF’s brands, teams, and markets ## The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12‑month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands‑on experience, including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. ## ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We celebrate the diversity of our associates and encourage everyone to bring their true selves to work every day. ## Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0011

unchangedJobs.title: Merchandiser Intern - EMEA Internship Program
unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Merchandiser Intern for Timberland. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you ready to support driving the footwear merchandising vision for our key wholesale partners across EMEA, ensuring the right products, assortments, and strategies reach the right consumers at the right time? You will support the execution of the footwear merchandising strategy for our wholesale partners, ensuring the right assortments, pricing, and consumer‑relevant stories are delivered to maximize brand impact and sell‑through. Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Merchandiser Intern: - Support the coordination of seasonal assortments for wholesale accounts. - Manage all sample orders, tracking, organization, and meetings preparation. - Update PLM data to keep product information accurate and current. - Maintain LP documents, ensuring assortments and SKUs stay up to date. - Prepare presentations for sell‑in meetings and internal product reviews. - Assist with pricing checks, SKUs validation, and seasonal readiness tasks. - Collaborate with cross‑functional teams on merchandising initiatives. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - Proactive mindset with the ability to take initiative and drive tasks forward. - Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills to support data analysis and presentation creation. - Open‑minded approach with curiosity for product, trends, and consumer behavior. - Effective collaborator who enjoys working within cross‑functional teams. - Fluent English communication in an international, fast‑paced environment. - Passion and sensitivity for fashion and footwear product . What’s in it for you At the end of the internship, you can expect to have a clear understanding of how to collaborate with the Global Footwear team in the US, how to partner effectively with EMEA Sales and Marketing, and how to deliver impactful product presentations to both internal sales teams and key strategic accounts. You will also develop strong footwear product knowledge through hands‑on learning, exposure to the full merchandising process, and direct coaching from industry experts, equipping you with the confidence and skills needed to grow within the footwear and fashion industry. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260205-0011

What’s in it for you At the end of the internship, you can expect to have a clear understanding of how to collaborate with the Global Footwear team in the US, how to partner effectively with EMEA Sales and Marketing, and how to deliver impactful product presentations to both internal sales teams and key strategic accounts. You will also develop strong footwear product knowledge through hands‑on learning, exposure to the full merchandising process, and direct coaching from industry experts, equipping you with the confidence and skills needed to grow within the footwear and fashion industry. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260205-0011

unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland a s Supply Chain, Data & Analytics Intern within Supply Chain Operations department. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Do you thrive on turning complex data into powerful insights that guide smarter, faster business decisions? VF Corporation is looking for an exceptio nal Supply Chain, Data & Analytics Intern t o join out team based in Stabio, Switzerland. In this role you will create analysis on various Supply Chain areas throughout the go-to-market process. You will also assist your manager in reporting and analyzing key Supply Chain KPIs to provide regular updates to Brand representatives and the Supply Chain Community and Leadership. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Supply Chain Data & Analytics Intern: - Create and implement analyses, reports, and dashboards to track Supply Chain performance and efficiency - Support the preparation of weekly/monthly reporting to be distributed to Supply Chain Community and to the Brands (i.e. On-Time Delivery to Customers, On-Time Stock Availability, Inventory Summary, DC Processing Performance, …) - Collaborate with Supply Chain functions and other business stakeholders to identify and formalize reporting/analytics requirements - Collaborate with Digital & Technology Analytics or BI functions to validate and bring live new Analytics models/solutions - Ensure the accuracy and transparency of all data shared with central functions and brands How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills - Basic knowledge of MS Power BI as visualization tool - IBM Cognos experience is a plus - Basic understanding of supply chain processes - Passionate for insight-driven thinking, can extract patterns from volumes of data to drive insights - Strong attention to details, and commitment to drive data accuracy - Good communication and presentation (including written) skills What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Main processes across different Supply Chain functions (i.e. DC Operations, Outbound Logistics, Customer Service, …) - Meaning of main KPIs measuring Supply Chain performance, how to calculate them and how to approach a deep dive analysis on the results - MS Power BI best practices to build interactive analysis covering both strategic and operational KPIs - Project management principles applied to Analytics solutions design and implementation You will also have the opportunity to interact and collaborate with colleagues in other regions (America or Asia), participating to international projects aimed to define a new Global Supply Chain KPIs Framework. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. unchangedJobs.title: Legal Intern - EMEA Internship Program
unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Legal Intern for VF EMEA Legal Department. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you ready to be introduced to the multifaceted world of corporate law within the retail industry in the EMEA region? VF is looking for a Legal Intern to join the Wholesale Business Area of the EMEA Legal Team based in Stabio, Switzerland. As a Legal Intern you will be offered a real-world exposure to in-house legal operations, including contract drafting and review and compliance checks. You will apply your academic knowledge in real-world legal challenges, promoting the synthesis of classroom teachings with practical experiences. You will work closely with legal professionals, expanding your professional network and understanding the importance of team dynamics in legal settings. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Legal Intern: - Performing legal research activities, including the review, study and analysis of relevant legislation, regulations and case law across the EMEA region. - Draft, review and negotiate various contracts (e.g. Partnership Stores Agreements, Distributorship Agreements, etc.), ensuring they align with VF Corporation’s interests and legal standards. - Assist other legal professionals in handling actual cases, observing and contributing to the resolution process. - Provide support to the team in the management, organization, and maintenance of archived documents and contracts. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - You are studying Law while beginning the internship or you have recently graduated in Law (after May 1st, 2026). - You are fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills. - You have curious mindset with passion for working with people. - You are eager to learn and face up new challenges. - First experiences such as other internships, extracurricular activities, is considered a plus. What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Legal Landscape of the Retail Industry: insights into the specific legal challenges and regulations. - Contract Drafting and Review: handling real contract negotiations and learning the art of compromise without compromising the company’s interests. - Stakeholder Engagement: the importance and methods of effectively communicating legal insights to non-legal stakeholders within the corporation. - Enhanced Professional Network: building relationships with legal professionals and leaders in the retail industry. - Applied Problem Solving: transitioning from theoretical knowledge to practical application in real-world business scenarios. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260218-0012

How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - You are studying Law while beginning the internship or you have recently graduated in Law (after May 1st, 2026). - You are fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills. - You have curious mindset with passion for working with people. - You are eager to learn and face up new challenges. - First experiences such as other internships, extracurricular activities, is considered a plus. What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Legal Landscape of the Retail Industry: insights into the specific legal challenges and regulations. - Contract Drafting and Review: handling real contract negotiations and learning the art of compromise without compromising the company’s interests. - Stakeholder Engagement: the importance and methods of effectively communicating legal insights to non-legal stakeholders within the corporation. - Enhanced Professional Network: building relationships with legal professionals and leaders in the retail industry. - Applied Problem Solving: transitioning from theoretical knowledge to practical application in real-world business scenarios. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. unchangedJobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
unchangedJobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Internal Audit Intern for VF Corporation. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Join an electric, evolving and growing team to support VF business both with assurance and advisory projects. Add value with your experience in improving VF processes and enriching Internal Audit program and department. Support your team members with the projects included in annual Internal Audit Plan and in the SOx program, push boundaries with new ideas, approached and proposals. Be proactive and ask, learn, challenge the status quo. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Internal Audit Intern: - Participate in person in delivering Internal audits, Sox related reviews and advisory projects both with reference to internal and external resources. - Confirm and complete the scope of the audit/review with the relevant owner or audit liaison. - Collaborate in creating or evolving audit programs, conduct the audit/review and participate in the report issuance for financial, operational, IT, Supply Chain or HR related audits and advisory projects. - Prepare the working papers ensuring that the audit objectives are met, and evidence supports the findings. - Prepare reports of findings and recommendations and present to Management. How you will make a difference We all have unique skills that we bring to work and celebrate every day. For this role, there are foundation skills you’ll need to succeed and excel: - Minimum of a Bachelor’s in one of the following fields of concentration – Accounting, Finance, Business, Data Analytics, IT/Information Security/Cyber Security, Supply Chain Management or similar - Certification preferred which may include CPA, CISA, CIA, and/or CFE - Technologically proficiency (i.e., Excel and PowerPoint required, Power BI preferred, any other Data Analytics tool is a plus) - Sense of urgency and strong organizational skills - Bring new ideas and challenge the status quo Skills that will make you succeed: - Strong presentation, verbal, and written skills - Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem solving - Strong verbal and written English skills, additional languages a plus - Great team working attitude and open mildness What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Meaningful projects work that drives business impact - Wide range of processes, from Finance to Supply Chain, IT and Operations - Regulatory requirements in different fields (Ex: SOx; CRSD; ISO9001 etc) - An holistic and eclectic way of collaborate and working with business leaders - How priorities are managed in a growing and evolving Internal Audit group The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0013

For this role, there are foundation skills you’ll need to succeed and excel: - Minimum of a Bachelor’s in one of the following fields of concentration – Accounting, Finance, Business, Data Analytics, IT/Information Security/Cyber Security, Supply Chain Management or similar - Certification preferred which may include CPA, CISA, CIA, and/or CFE - Technologically proficiency (i.e., Excel and PowerPoint required, Power BI preferred, any other Data Analytics tool is a plus) - Sense of urgency and strong organizational skills - Bring new ideas and challenge the status quo Skills that will make you succeed: - Strong presentation, verbal, and written skills - Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem solving - Strong verbal and written English skills, additional languages a plus - Great team working attitude and open mildness What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Meaningful projects work that drives business impact - Wide range of processes, from Finance to Supply Chain, IT and Operations - Regulatory requirements in different fields (Ex: SOx; CRSD; ISO9001 etc) - An holistic and eclectic way of collaborate and working with business leaders - How priorities are managed in a growing and evolving Internal Audit group The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0013
